pattern: Three-way turn-taking generalizes the two-gate handoff: the zero thread is the dispatcher, opening the odd or even gate based on the number it just preceded.
hints: The zero thread runs n times, but odd and even each run only about n/2 — size their loops before thinking about locks.
Zero should decide who goes next: after printing 0 for the i-th time, open odd's gate when i is odd, even's when i is even.
Both number threads hand control back to zero after printing — three semaphores, zero's initialized open.

edge_inputs.description: n = 1 — the even thread must exit without printing

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    return [rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 20)))]

oracle_python:
import threading


class ZeroEvenOdd:
    def __init__(self, n):
        self.n = n
        self.state = "zero"
        self.i = 1
        self.cv = threading.Condition()

    def zero(self, printNumber):
        for _ in range(self.n):
            with self.cv:
                self.cv.wait_for(lambda: self.state == "zero")
                printNumber(0)
                self.state = "odd" if self.i % 2 == 1 else "even"
                self.cv.notify_all()

    def even(self, printNumber):
        for _ in range(self.n // 2):
            with self.cv:
                self.cv.wait_for(lambda: self.state == "even")
                printNumber(self.i)
                self.i += 1
                self.state = "zero"
                self.cv.notify_all()

    def odd(self, printNumber):
        for _ in range((self.n + 1) // 2):
            with self.cv:
                self.cv.wait_for(lambda: self.state == "odd")
                printNumber(self.i)
                self.i += 1
                self.state = "zero"
                self.cv.notify_all()
